taskTitle: Identify and eliminate any practices that contradict Tawhid
subtaskTitle: Identify any reliance on amulets, charms, or talismans
csQueryPrompt: For a MILOS subtask titled 'Identify any reliance on amulets, charms, or talismans' under the parent task 'Identify and eliminate any practices that contradict Tawhid' (module: faith_shahada_core), propose exactly 2 NotebookLM queries to a Quran/Hadith primary-source corpus: (1) Quran-biased — name likely primary ayat that directly or contextually anchor this subtask; (2) Hadith-biased — name likely Sahih hadith (Bukhari/Muslim preferred) that directly or contextually anchor this subtask. Return the queries inside a <queries></queries> block as a numbered list, each one sentence, ready to paste into a retrieval system. Do NOT answer the queries — query formulation only.
extractedQueries: What are the primary ayat, such as Surah Az-Zumar 39:38 and Surah Al-An'am 6:17, that establish that only Allah can avert harm or bring benefit, contextually anchoring the theological prohibition against relying on amulets and charms?

Which authentic narrations, particularly those discussing "Tama'im" (amulets) or the command to remove string necklaces from camels found in Sahih al-Bukhari and Sahih Muslim, explicitly identify the use of talismans as a contradiction to Tawhid?
